_note: Compaction ratchet floor for track-coach's living docs. One cap file, two readers. gate h (tests/test_compaction_locks.py, the s73 his-law port) reads style_errors_max / redundancy_open_max; gate r (tests/test_ratchet_lock.py, the live-spec 2.1.0 turnkey kit) reads max_style_errors / max_redundancy_open / max_waivers. The two spellings carry the same numbers on purpose. All caps only RATCHET DOWN: lowering a number is a normal commit; RAISING one is a deliberate, named edit that says why in the same commit (and gate r's lock also guards the raise in tests/test_ratchet_lock.py).
redundancy_note: The 29 open pairs are the spec's DELIBERATE cross-section restatements (an invariant plus its section narrative; a decisions-ledger entry plus its surface application; per-story fences), each carrying a unique code/date/caveat — a Fable triage on 2026-07-16 confirmed every one is load-bearing, zero true duplicates. The cap stops NEW duplication; it does not ask these to merge.
